title: Counterfactual undercut-value features instead of only finish-feasibility rules
summary: Official F1 strategy sources repeatedly frame pit timing around undercut strength, pit-lane loss, warm-up, tyre offset, and clean-air windows.
rationale: Official F1 strategy sources repeatedly frame pit timing around undercut strength, pit-lane loss, warm-up, tyre offset, and clean-air windows. The current best code mostly models whether a fresh tyre can finish, not whether stopping now is tactically worth more than waiting one lap.
implementation_hint: Build race-safe proxies for `expected_undercut_gain_now`: estimate stint degradation slope from prior laps only, estimate race-specific pit-loss / out-lap penalty from earlier pit events in the same race or historical races only, and combine with clean-air / traffic proxies from lap-relative pace and position context. Add features like `stay_out_cost_1lap`, `pit_now_net_gain`, and `pit_now_vs_next_lap_margin`.
expected_effect: Should improve ranking inside the ambiguous mid-window rows where many laps are mechanically feasible but only a few are strategically optimal to pit now.
risk: Easy to leak if pit-loss or warm-up templates use future laps from the same race; compute from past-only events and validate by `Race_Year` or stricter race holdout.
